sidebar.title: A. J. Aragón
sidebar.description_1: Web Applications Developer specialized in .NET, Laravel and Angular.
sidebar.description_2: Student of SOLID principles, design patterns and best practices.

welcome.title: Welcome, I'm Andrés Aragón:
welcome.text: Web application developer passionate about building innovative and efficient solutions. I consider myself a calm, thoughtful, and highly committed professional. I am genuinely invested in continuous learning, constant improvement, and delivering high-quality work. My approach is careful and patient: I analyze problems thoroughly, ask questions when needed, and prioritize robust, long-term solutions over quick fixes. I enjoy working in teams that value respect, knowledge sharing, and a shared commitment to building quality products.

projects.title: Projects
projects.intro: In this section you will find a brief description of the projects I have completed:

solitaire.title: Solitaire Game
solitaire.description: Classic card game developed with HTML, CSS and JavaScript.
solitaire.details_1: The purpose of this project was to practice programming logic. It was an idea that forced me to maintain a rule-based context. That's why I had to manage the game state and iteration through DOM events.
solitaire.details_2: I learned to handle complex events like drag and drop and to create simple animations to improve gameplay; as well as the importance of defining requirements before coding to reduce iterative errors.
solitaire.details_3: With what I know today, I would better organize the logic solution and go step by step: I would structure the code clearly separating the game logic (model) from DOM manipulation (view), for example using classes for the deck, suit and column, which would facilitate testing and changes.

library.title: Pyramid Library
library.description: Creative frontend layout and design for a fictional library.
library.details_1: This project was an exercise focused on advanced CSS layout and improving user experience through small interactions, prioritizing visual creativity and fluidity over complex logic.
library.details_2: This project allowed me to delve into precise layout control with Flexbox/Grid, strategic use of CSS transitions and animations to guide attention, and subtle JavaScript integration.

zoo.title: Senda Animal
zoo.description: Informative website about a fictional zoo, developed with Angular, SCSS.
zoo.details: This project is currently under development. I am focusing on learning how to structure an Angular application using components and services. I intend to apply good development practices and use design patterns.

minerals.title: Minerals Analyzer
minerals.description: An Angular application to practice and demonstrate architecture and code best practices.
minerals.details_1: This project started as an exercise in a course. Its main objective was to serve as a testing ground to concretely apply software design principles and clean architecture using Angular.
minerals.details_2: I developed the application focusing on how to organize code in a maintainable way. I tried to apply specific design patterns (like Dependency Injection, Services or Repository) and SOLID principles to clearly separate responsibilities. For example, I created independent services to handle business logic and components dedicated only to displaying information. I also experimented with interface design to make it functional.
minerals.details_3: This exercise made me see the difference between 'it works' and 'it's well built'. I learned in practice why it's useful to separate logic into small independent parts: to be able to change one part without breaking everything else.

hotel.title: Hotel Argyros
hotel.description: Una aplicación en Angular y .NET para practicar y demostrar arquitectura y buenas prácticas de código.
hotel.details_1: Este proyecto se encuentra actualmente en desarrollo. Pretendo demostrar mis conocimientos tanto en todo lo relacionado con las bases de datos, como el backend y el frontend.

english.title: English
english.level_1: I understand technical, development and literary texts without problems, and conversations about everyday and technical topics.
english.level_2: I can maintain conversations with relative fluency and I write clear texts both everyday and technical.

french.title: French
french.level_1: I understand technical, development and literary texts without problems; although I find it a bit harder to understand conversations.
french.level_2: I can write clear texts both everyday and technical; but I find it harder to maintain fluent conversations.
